card_description: Aristotle (Greek (Aristos + telos): "Best purpose") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the classical tradition, Aristotle is philosopher of substance and form; plato's student. PKD engages with Aristotelian categories when discussing substance and form. Appears in 3 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Aristotle. Context: "Dick identifies the "Holy Spirit" as a collective immortal Light Body that re-entered him to stimulate his "Third Soul" (Aristotelian/immortal)."

linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_26.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_54.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies his novel *Ubik* as an accurate depiction of the "unscrambling" of reality into Form I (Life/Real) and Form II (Dead/Hologram). He posits that Form I (Zebra) is a "supralunar" invader
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_142.txt
